task.prompt: Write a function `bsearch(xs, target)` that returns the index of `target` in the sorted list `xs`, or -1 if not present.

gold_spec.goal: Locate `target` in a sorted list using O(log n) comparisons; return its index, or -1 if absent.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: an index i such that xs[i] == target, or -1 if no such index exists

preconditions: xs is sorted in non-decreasing order (caller's responsibility — not verified)
postconditions: if target in xs then xs[result] == target; otherwise result == -1
edge_cases: target smaller than all elements returns -1
target larger than all elements returns -1
duplicates: any matching index may be returned (caller must not assume leftmost / rightmost)
single-element list: returns 0 if it matches, -1 otherwise
acceptance_criteria: for any sorted list xs and any int target, returns an index i with xs[i] == target if one exists, else -1
runs in O(log n) time (binary, not linear, search)
